text:
Market today: Iran deal + oil stability is giving the market a relief bid Monday. That kind of geopolitical exhale tends to lift risk assets broadly — good for tech and chips in the short run, but it's not a fundamental catalyst for your names.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 14.66% and it's your largest position; on margin that hole is real and getting uncomfortable. Watch the $336 stop hard. PLTR is down 11.08% and already flagged SELL — if you haven't trimmed, the clock is ticking. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history; it's only up 6.62% but can swing violently — margin amplifies any reversal fast.

Watch: 1) AVGO — does it stabilize or keep sliding toward $336? That stop is your line in the sand. 2) Broad tech sentiment — if SPY/QQQ fade from today's relief rally, your chip-heavy book gets hit first on margin calls.

Bottom line: Protect AVGO and cut PLTR — two bleeding positions on margin is how accounts get wrecked.
